task: R6-B — Healthcare practice state privacy add-on (Florida patient records + FIPA)
disclaimer: Operational compliance research — not legal advice. Florida patient-record and data-security rules may exceed HIPAA for certain facts.
policy: Public statute sources only; gaps explicit.

cited_facts.text: Health care practitioners must furnish copies of reports and records relating to examination or treatment upon request of the patient or legal representative, without conditioning on payment of service fees.

Records may not be furnished to persons other than the patient, legal representative, or other treating practitioners except upon written patient authorization, subject to listed exceptions (subpoena, fraud investigation, etc.).
Absent specific written release, use of patient information for solicitation or marketing the sale of goods or services is prohibited.
Records owners must develop and implement policies to protect confidentiality and security of medical records; employees must be trained; disclosure logs must be maintained.

data_security_breach.label: F.S. 501.171 — Florida Information Protection Act (health info in PI)

regulator.agency: Florida Senate — Statutes / FL Department of Legal Affairs

cited_facts.text: Covered entities must take reasonable measures to protect and secure electronic data containing personal information; 'personal information' includes medical history, condition, treatment, diagnosis, and health insurance identifiers.

Breach affecting 500+ Florida individuals: notify Department of Legal Affairs within 30 days; notify affected individuals within 30 days unless law enforcement delay or documented low-risk determination.
